title: Investment in Genomic Tools for Salmonella Source Attribution Testing
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Salmonella     remains     one     of     the     leading     causes     of     foodborne     illness     globally  ,   causing     significant     morbidity  ,   mortality  ,   and     economic     losses  .   Traditional     microbiological     and     serotyping     methods     often     lack     the     resolution     to     pinpoint     outbreak     sources  ,   leading     to     delays   in   response     and     ineffective     interventions  . Investment in   genomic     tools  ,   such   as   Whole     Genome     Sequencing   (WGS)   and     metagenomic     approaches  ,   offers     a     powerful     solution     to     improve     source     attribution  . By   enabling     detailed     genetic     characterization  ,   these     tools     can     identify     transmission     pathways  ,   link     outbreaks     to     specific     food     sources  ,   and   monitor   the     emergence     of     antimicrobial     resistance  .   Strategic     investments   in   infrastructure  ,   training  ,   and   data-  sharing     platforms     will     strengthen     national     surveillance     systems  ,   support     timely     public     health     interventions  ,   and     ultimately     reduce     the     burden     of     Salmonella  -related     foodborne     illness  .
